goal: information.* cleanup: drop section_comments entirely (style smell agents fix readily); rename information.magic_literals → structural.magic_literals (it's a structural readability signal, not Halstead-family); port the relocated rule to v2 view-native

summary.title: information.* cleanup + magic_literals view-native port
summary.what: (1) Dropped information.section_comments entirely — divider comments inside function bodies are a style smell agents readily clean up; doesn't validate slop's structural-debt thesis. Deleted rule file, legacy kernel, tests, config entries; added entry to slop._compat.REMOVED_RULES so old configs fail cleanly. (2) Relocated information.magic_literals → structural.magic_literals — magic literals are a structural readability signal (missing parameterization), not a Halstead-derived information measure. The information.* category is now Halstead-only (volume + difficulty), which makes its name honest. Old configs resolve via slop._compat.LEGACY_RULE_NAMES. (3) Ported the relocated rule to v2 view-native: new slop.language.ast.Literal StrEnum + Language.numeric_literal_nodes() classmethod with declarations on all 11 grammars. run_magic_literals_v2 walks each callable's body, finds numeric literal nodes, parses text (hex/oct/bin/separators/suffixes), excludes trivials, counts distinct non-trivials. Parity verified vs legacy magic_literals_kernel across all 11 supported languages. 493 tests pass.
summary.why: The information.* category lumped together two genuinely different concerns: Halstead-derived information theory (volume, difficulty) and rules that just happened to share a function-body-walking shape (magic_literals, section_comments). The user's standing 'structural debt not style' rule and the desire for an honest category name pointed at both motions: kill the style rule, relocate the structural one. The v2 port piggybacks because the rule was already going to be touched.
summary.user_goal: information.* cleanup: drop section_comments entirely (style smell agents fix readily); rename information.magic_literals → structural.magic_literals (it's a structural readability signal, not Halstead-family); port the relocated rule to v2 view-native

decisions.chose: Kill section_comments rather than port it. Removed kernel, rule, tests, config — full deletion via REMOVED_RULES.
decisions.rationale: Standing memory feedback: 'rules that catch style smells agents readily fix should be cut entirely; only ship rules that drive non-trivial review action'. Section dividers inside functions are exactly that — agents clean them up trivially; the rule never produced action-worthy findings.

decisions.chose: Relocate magic_literals to structural.* rather than keep under information.*.
decisions.rationale: Magic numeric literals signal embedded domain logic that should live in named constants — a structural readability concern (missing parameterization). Halstead information theory measures (V, D) are categorically different. The original information.* grouping was a stretch; the v2.0 break is a good moment to honest the boundary.

decisions.chose: Add slop.language.ast.Literal as a new per-category enum rather than fold numeric literal node types into existing enums.
decisions.rationale: Literals are a distinct category from Identifier/Callable/Scope/Operator. Adding a new enum (currently 16 members, all numeric) preserves the 'each enum is one semantic family' shape the user established when splitting the flat Node enum. Future string-literal or bool-literal support extends Literal naturally.

decisions.chose: Parse literal text in the rule, not on the AST node directly.
decisions.rationale: Tree-sitter exposes raw text only; values like '0xff_ff' or '1.5e10' or '100L' need explicit parsing. The rule owns the value-extraction policy; the view stays pure shape-traversal. _parse_numeric is shared utility that handles hex/oct/bin prefixes, numeric separators, and type suffixes across all 11 languages.

decisions.chose: Build a parity sweep against legacy magic_literals_kernel before adding tests, then hand-compute test values to match.
decisions.rationale: Cross-language algorithms have subtle edge cases. Running the sweep first surfaces any divergence; then tests can lock down the verified values. Same approach used for cyclomatic/cognitive ports.

decisions.chose: Delete the legacy magic_literals tests (tests/test_rules/test_magic_literals.py) rather than port them.
decisions.rationale: The legacy tests exercise the (root, RuleConfig, SlopConfig) signature against the legacy kernel. v2 needs Structure-based fixtures. Rewriting the tests for v2 was cheaper than retrofitting. The legacy kernel itself stays in _structural/ as a survivor (multi-week kernel-retirement is a separate sweep).

review_notes: 493 tests pass total. Test-count change: -11 (delete legacy magic_literals tests) -7 (delete section_comments mixed-file tests across c/cpp/ruby) -16 (delete test_section_comments.py) +12 (add v2 magic_literals tests) = net -22. Coverage is denser per remaining test (v2 tests are tighter and cover multi-language parity in fewer cases).
Cross-language parity sweep: 11 of 11 languages match legacy on count. Julia's name extraction was BETTER in v2 (correct 'discount' vs legacy 'anonymous'); not a regression.
Port status: 5 view-native (cyclomatic, cognitive, god_module, imposters, magic_literals), 26 shim-wrapped. Down from 27 because section_comments is gone entirely.
information.* now contains only volume + difficulty — Halstead-only, honest.
Compat shim handles both motions: information.magic_literals → structural.magic_literals (rename); information.section_comments → REMOVED_RULES with reason.

architectural_claims: information.* is a Halstead-only category. Future Halstead-derived measures (effort, time-to-program) belong here; anything else lives under structural.* or its own category.
Numeric literal node-type strings live in slop.language.ast.Literal (StrEnum). Per-grammar selection via Language.numeric_literal_nodes() classmethod.
Literal-text parsing is a rule concern, not a view concern. The view exposes raw AST nodes; rules that need numeric values parse text themselves.
behavioral_changes: information.section_comments rule is gone. Old configs referencing it will error via REMOVED_RULES.
information.magic_literals → structural.magic_literals rename. Old configs resolve via LEGACY_RULE_NAMES.
The magic_literals rule runs view-native; identical distinct-count semantics to legacy across all 11 supported languages.
migration_notes: Users with [rules.information.magic_literals] in .slop.toml: rename to [rules.structural.magic_literals]. The legacy name is accepted via compat shim but will warn.
Users with [rules.information.section_comments]: remove the section. The rule no longer exists.
